questions.question: A motorboat whose speed in still water is $18 \text{ km/h}$ takes $1$ hour more to go $24 \text{ km}$ upstream than to return downstream to the same spot. Form a quadratic equation to represent this situation and find the speed of the stream.

steps: Let the speed of the stream be $x \text{ km/h}$.
Then, the speed of the boat upstream is $(18 - x) \text{ km/h}$ and downstream is $(18 + x) \text{ km/h}$.
Using the formula $\text{Time} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Speed}}$, the time taken upstream is $\frac{24}{18 - x}$ and downstream is $\frac{24}{18 + x}$.
According to the problem, the difference in time is $1$ hour: $\frac{24}{18 - x} - \frac{24}{18 + x} = 1$.
Simplify the equation to standard form: $24(18 + x) - 24(18 - x) = (18 - x)(18 + x) \Rightarrow 48x = 324 - x^2$.
Solve the quadratic equation using the quadratic formula or middle-term splitting: $(x + 54)(x - 6) = 0$.
Since speed cannot be negative, $x = 6 \text{ km/h}$.

retrieved_sources: Chapter_3 (Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables).pdf
